explanations.4: The -f operator is PowerShell's string formatting tool; it replaces {0} and {1} with the values that follow it, similar to printf in other languages.
explanations.7: New-Object creates an instance of a .NET class; we're making a SQL connection object and passing the connection string as an argument to its constructor.
explanations.9: CreateCommand() is a method that builds a command object attached to this specific connection, so the command knows which database to talk to.
explanations.13: ExecuteReader() runs the query and returns a special object that lets us read results one row at a time instead of all at once.
explanations.19: The while loop calls SqlDataReader.Read() which grabs the next row each time; when there are no more rows, Read() returns false and the loop stops.
explanations.20: SqlDataReader works like a dictionary where you can access columns by their name in quotes, like ['LastName'], to get the value from the current row.
